channel.title: NYT > U.S. > Politics
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Takeaways From Tuesday’s Primary Elections in Maine and South Carolina
item.description: Graham Platner won the Democratic primary for Senate in Maine and quickly turned his populist message against Susan Collins, the Republican incumbent.
item.dc:creator: Kellen Browning
item.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times
item.media:description: Graham Platner in Blue Hill, Maine, after winning the state’s Democratic primary for Senate on Tuesday.

item.title: Republicans in Maine Governor’s Race Head to Ranked-Choice Runoff
item.description: State election officials will confirm the winner in the next week or two, after a counting process that involves multiple rounds of elimination.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Russell
item.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times
item.media:description: Voters in Augusta, Maine, filling out their ballots on Tuesday.

item.title: Democrats Face Another Long Wait for Results in Maine Swing District
item.description: No candidate cleared 50 percent in the Democratic primary race to replace Representative Jared Golden, sending the race into ranked-choice voting.
item.dc:creator: Bayliss Wagner and Tim Balk

item.title: Nevada Attorney General Wins Democratic Nomination for Governor
item.description: Aaron Ford easily outpaced a Washoe County commissioner, Alexis Hill, for the nod to take on Gov. Joe Lombardo in November.
item.dc:creator: Reis Thebault
item.media:credit: Jae C. Hong/Associated Press
item.media:description: Attorney General Aaron Ford of Nevada celebrating in Las Vegas after winning the Democratic nomination for governor on Tuesday.

item.title: Republican Primary for Open Congressional Seat in South Carolina Heads to Runoff
item.description: The largely coastal district has voted overwhelmingly Republican in recent elections. Representative Nancy Mace ran unsuccessfully for governor instead of seeking re-election to the seat.
item.dc:creator: Nick Corasaniti and Bayliss Wagner
item.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times
item.media:description: The Republican primary for South Carolina’s First Congressional District advanced to a runoff on Tuesday.

item.title: Trump’s Sharp Turn on China: Embracing It as a Peer Power
item.description: President Trump’s warming with Xi Jinping of China, a leader he admires, has ignited anxieties in Washington and across Asia.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: The U.S. and Chinese governments rolled out a new phrase, “constructive strategic stability,” during President Trump’s meeting in Beijing last month with the leader of China, Xi Jinping.

item.title: Trump Says Bill Pulte Will Take Over as Intelligence Chief on June 19
item.description: The president also plans to keep Mr. Pulte as the head of a federal housing agency, heightening tension with Congress and complicating the renewal of a powerful spying authority.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron
item.category: Office of the Director of National Intelligence
item.media:credit: Eric Lee/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump has suggested that Bill Pulte might use the temporary appointment as director of national intelligence as an opportunity to purge the office of perceived political opponents.

item.title: Platner Wins Maine Senate Primary After a Turbulent Stretch of His Bid
item.description: Graham Platner has faced new scrutiny over his personal history, but his Democratic primary victory sets up a high-stakes race against Senator Susan Collins, a Republican.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein
item.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times
item.media:description: Graham Platner’s primary victory was long expected after his main Democratic opponent, Gov. Janet Mills, suspended her campaign in late April.

item.title: Evette and Wilson Advance to Runoff in South Carolina Republican Primary for Governor
item.description: Lt. Gov. Pamela Evette and Alan Wilson, the state attorney general, now head to a runoff in the Republican contest for governor. President Trump vowed to “fight” for Ms. Evette in the second round.
item.dc:creator: Eduardo Medina, Emily Cochrane and Nick Madigan
item.media:credit: Meg Kinnard/Associated Press
item.media:description: Lt. Gov. Pamela Evette of South Carolina at a campaign rally on Monday.

item.title: Sanders-Backed Progressive Advances in California Swing District Against Valadao
item.description: Randy Villegas will face the incumbent, David Valadao, a Republican who has survived numerous challenges in a competitive district.
item.dc:creator: Kellen Browning
item.media:credit: Jon Kopaloff/Getty Images For Connor Treacy
item.media:description: Randy Villegas, a progressive backed by Senator Bernie Sanders of Vermont, defeated Dr. Jasmeet Bains in the Democratic primary in California’s 22nd Congressional District. He will face Representative David Valadao, the Republican incumbent.

item.title: Lindsey Graham Wins Republican Senate Primary in South Carolina
item.description: Mr. Graham, a four-term incumbent, is the favorite to win the general election in a solidly Republican state.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Mazzei
item.media:credit: Demetrius Freeman for The New York Times
item.media:description: Senator Lindsey Graham, Republican of South Carolina, in Washington in February.

item.title: Navy Admiral Fired by Hegseth Advances to Runoff to Replace Mace
item.description: Nancy Lacore would face a tough fall election in a district drawn in 2021 to be more Republican. She is seeking to replace Representative Nancy Mace, who ran unsuccessfully for governor instead of running for re-election.
item.dc:creator: Nick Corasaniti and Bayliss Wagner
item.media:credit: Shawn Thew/EPA, via Shutterstock
item.media:description: Nancy Lacore, a former U.S. Navy admiral, is seeking a congressional seat in South Carolina after Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth fired her last year.

item.title: Rep. Kevin Kiley, Independent, to Face Richard Pan in California House Race
item.description: Mr. Kiley dropped his Republican affiliation earlier this year. The newly drawn Sixth Congressional District is widely considered to be favorable for Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Medina
item.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times, Rich Pedroncelli/Associated Press
item.media:description: Representative Kevin Kiley and Richard Pan will compete in November.

item.title: 5 Things to Know About Paul LePage, Republican House Candidate in Maine
item.description: Mr. LePage served two terms as governor of Maine and was known for his incendiary rhetoric and his vetoing of legislation of all sorts.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Robinson
item.media:credit: Taylor Coester/Reuters
item.media:description: “I was Donald Trump before Donald Trump became popular,” Paul Mr. LePage said in 2016, after endorsing Mr. Trump for president.

item.title: House defies Johnson and passes Democratic-led labor bill
item.description: Twenty G.O.P lawmakers broke with Speaker Mike Johnson and joined Democrats in backing a bill to amend the National Labor Relations Act.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Diaz
item.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times
item.media:description: The vote on Tuesday was the latest reflection of Speaker Mike Johnson’s weak hold on his narrow majority.

item.title: 5 Things to Know About Graham Platner, Democratic Senate Candidate in Maine
item.description: Mr. Platner, an oyster farmer running for a Senate seat on a progressive platform, has drawn a large following among Democrats in Maine. His campaign has also surfaced damaging reports about his personal history.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times
item.media:description: Graham Platner in April. He is expected to face Susan Collins, a five-term Republican senator, in the general election.

item.title: 5 Things to Know About Susan Collins, Republican Senate Candidate in Maine
item.description: Ms. Collins, the longest-serving Republican woman in Senate history, is also her party’s most vulnerable incumbent this year.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.media:credit: Eric Lee/The New York Times
item.media:description: Senator Susan Collins of Maine has long cultivated a reputation for independence, but she has backed the Trump administration on some important votes.

item.title: Doris Matsui to Face Mai Vang in General Election for Sacramento House Seat
item.description: Mai Vang, a progressive member of the Sacramento City Council, will challenge Ms. Matsui, the 81-year-old incumbent, in the general election.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Rosenhall
item.media:credit: Mike Kai Chen for The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Doris Matsui at the state Democratic convention in February.

item.title: When to Expect Results in Maine, Nevada and Beyond
item.description: Four states have primaries on Tuesday. Election officials predict most results will come in late in the evening, though mail-in ballots may delay things.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Robinson
item.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times
item.media:description: People check in to vote in Portland, Maine, on Tuesday.

item.title: The Likely Platner-Collins Matchup Is Set to Be Hugely Expensive
item.description: Both Senate candidates in Maine have been strong fund-raisers, and allied super PACs and dark-money groups are mobilizing for their showdown.
item.dc:creator: Matt Zdun and Theodore Schleifer
item.media:credit: Joe Raedle/Getty Images, Michael A. McCoy for The New York Time
item.media:description: Both parties are expected to spend heavily on the likely race between Graham Platner, a Democrat, and Senator Susan Collins, a Republican.

item.title: How Ranked-Choice Voting Works in Maine
item.description: Proponents say the practice reduces partisanship, while critics say it is confusing and expensive.
item.dc:creator: Nick Corasaniti
item.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times
item.media:description: Voters lining up to cast their votes on Tuesday at the Kennebunkport fire station.

item.title: South Carolina Voters Are Choosing Nominees to Fill an Open Seat in Congress
item.description: Representative Nancy Mace’s run for governor created the opening in the coastal First Congressional District. Republicans are expected to hold it, but one Democrat has proved to be a prolific fund-raiser.
item.dc:creator: Nick Corasaniti
item.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times
item.media:description: The race to replace Representative Nancy Race, a Republican from South Carolina, is one of several G.O.P. primary contests Tuesday that are expected to produce new members of Congress.

item.title: G.M. Plans to Develop Energy Storage Batteries as E.V. Sales Flag
item.description: The carmaker will follow Tesla in making components for large batteries used by electric utilities, data centers and other businesses to handle fluctuations in power supply and demand.
item.dc:creator: Jack Ewing
item.media:credit: Rebecca Cook/Reuters
item.media:description: The main active ingredient in the storage batteries that General Motors plans to produce is sodium, which is cheaper and easier to process than lithium.

item.title: Blanche Was Once Seen as Tempering Trump’s Tactics. Now He’s All In.
item.description: The ascendancy of Todd Blanche shows how the practices that were initially deemed out of bounds even in President Trump’s Justice Department seem to be the order of the day.
item.dc:creator: Glenn Thrush
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: Todd Blanche, the acting attorney general, during a cabinet meeting at the White House in May.

item.title: U.S. Strikes Iran After Trump Vows to Retaliate for Downed Helicopter
item.description: The retaliatory U.S. attacks came on Tuesday as Israeli forces pounded southern Lebanon while targeting Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt, Maggie Haberman, Euan Ward and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times
item.media:description: “The United States must, of necessity, respond to this attack,” President Trump said on social media before the strikes.

item.title: Maine’s Primary Ballot Is Crowded With Members of Political Dynasties
item.description: Among other candidates with ties to prominent officials, a senator’s son and a Bush scion are running for governor.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Russell
item.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: Pro-Israel Group Pushes for Haley Stevens in Michigan Senate Race
item.description: The main super PAC tied to the influential American Israel Public Affairs Committee released its first ads backing Ms. Stevens, a moderate congresswoman locked in a tight three-way primary.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Medina and Theodore Schleifer
item.media:credit: Sarah Rice/Getty Images
item.media:description: Representative Haley Stevens of Michigan has been a vocal supporter of Israel in the past.

item.title: Traditional Republicans Clash With MAGA Wing in Nevada House Primary
item.description: At stake is the seat held by Representative Mark Amodei, a Republican in northern Nevada who is retiring from his reliably red district.
item.dc:creator: Kellen Browning

item.title: Several women who dated Graham Platner recall his ‘unsettling’ behavior.
item.dc:creator: Katie Glueck and Lisa Lerer
item.media:credit: Gabe Souza for The New York Times
item.media:description: Graham Platner, a combat veteran, has spoken openly about his past struggles with post-traumatic stress disorder, depression and drinking, and has said his behavior years ago does not reflect who he is today.

item.title: In Maine, a Senate race provokes complex emotions for voters.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk, Alicia Anstead and Kathleen Tomaselli
item.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times
item.media:description: Graham Platner’s campaign has drawn large crowds of enthusiastic supporters in Maine, but many Democrats are anxious about his candidacy.

item.title: Democrats Accuse Republicans of Meddling in Maine’s Second Congressional District Primary
item.description: A mysterious group has spent $500,000 to elevate a progressive candidate in the Democratic primary race for a key House seat.
item.dc:creator: Bayliss Wagner
item.media:credit: John Tully for The New York Times
item.media:description: Voting on Tuesday in Lewiston, Maine. The city is part of the state’s Second Congressional District, which is expected to be competitive in November.

item.title: House Passes G.O.P.’s $70 Billion Immigration Bill
item.description: The vote gave final passage to Republicans’ megabill to fund immigration enforcement through the remainder of President Trump’s term, clearing it for his signature.
item.dc:creator: Catie Edmondson
item.media:credit: Ashley Wu/The New York Times

item.title: Here’s what to know about how The Times reports results and race calls.
item.dc:creator: Nick Corasaniti

item.title: U.S. Strikes Iran After Trump Vowed to Retaliate for Downed Army Helicopter
item.description: President Trump vowed to retaliate for what he said was Iran’s downing of an Apache helicopter. near the Strait of Hormuz. The pilot and gunner were rescued.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Reuters
item.media:description: Vessels anchored in the Strait of Hormuz near Musandam, Oman.

item.title: U.S. and Iran Zero In on Four Nuclear Issues in Talks
item.description: With the cease-fire proving tenuous, negotiations between the two nations are in flux, but have advanced to outline potential paths forward on difficult questions about Iran’s nuclear program.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: Tehran this week. Hard-liners in Iran could derail what American officials have described as the hazy outlines of an accord.

item.title: A South Carolina race tests the strength of a Trump endorsement.
item.dc:creator: Eduardo Medina and Emily Cochrane
item.media:credit: Caroline Gutman for The New York Times
item.media:description: The candidates for governor in South Carolina went to extraordinary lengths to win President Trump’s endorsement.

item.title: Watchdog Group Skeptical of Vows to End Trump Administration’s $1.8 Billion Fund
item.description: Two sets of lawyers noted that while senior department officials said the fund would not move forward, they declined to offer assurances that this would hold in the future.
item.dc:creator: Alan Feuer
item.category: House Select Committee to Investigate the January 6th Attack
Citizens for Responsibility and Ethics in Washington
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Justice Department has promised both in writing and in sworn testimony that it will not proceed with the plan.

item.title: At 10,000 consecutive votes and counting, Collins holds a Senate record.
item.description: The Maine Republican, who is in the middle of a tumultuous re-election race, became the first senator in history to reach the threshold without missing a vote.
item.dc:creator: Carl Hulse
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Senator Susan Collins, Republican of Maine, at the U.S. Capitol.

item.title: Lindsey Graham’s Allies Spend Big to Ward Off a Republican Challenger
item.description: The Republican senator from South Carolina has a well-funded primary challenger who has a chance of forcing him into a runoff election.
item.dc:creator: Matt Zdun and Theodore Schleifer
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times, Alex Martin, via Imagn Images
item.media:description: Senator Lindsey Graham, left, Mark Lynch, right.

item.title: Bill Gates Said to Have Hired Ex-House Oversight Chief to Advise on Epstein Testimony
item.description: Bill Gates, the Microsoft co-founder, has retained Jake Greenberg, the former top investigative counsel to the House Oversight Committee.
item.dc:creator: Annie Karni
item.category: House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform
item.media:credit: Calla Kessler for The New York Times
item.media:description: Bill Gates has been preparing to testify privately in the House Oversight Committee’s investigation of Jeffrey Epstein.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein and Nick Corasaniti

item.title: A Challenge in the U.S.-Iran Talks: Both Sides Demand Victory
item.description: Washington and Tehran would need to defend any potential deal as a win for their side. And each has a leader whose approach to talks is vexing mediators.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon, Farnaz Fassihi and Tyler Pager
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: A rally in Tehran on Monday after Iran and Israel exchanged fire.

item.title: Democratic Group Kicks Off $50 Million Midterm Blitz in Conservative Strongholds
item.description: American Bridge is hoping that Republicans are vulnerable in parts of the country that had been exceedingly tough terrain for Democrats in recent elections.
item.dc:creator: Shane Goldmacher
item.media:credit: Michael A. McCoy for The New York Times
item.media:description: American Bridge is kicking off a roughly $50 million advertising campaign targeting Republicans in more than a dozen House districts and four Senate races.

item.title: Maine Elections Test Democrats’ Support for Platner: What to Watch in Today’s Primaries
item.description: Any indication of tepid support for Graham Platner, a Democratic candidate for Senate in Maine, will be seen as a warning sign about his chances against Senator Susan Collins, the Republican incumbent.
item.dc:creator: Kellen Browning
item.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times
item.media:description: Voting on Tuesday in Portland, Maine. The state’s Senate race is one of this year’s top midterm contests.

item.title: The Ex-Mayor of Arcadia, Her Boyfriend and the Chinese Government
item.description: Eileen Wang pleaded guilty to acting as a foreign agent. But what could Beijing want from the mayor of a small California city known as the “Chinese Beverly Hills”?
item.dc:creator: Amy Qin
item.media:credit: William Liang/Associated Press
item.media:description: Eileen Wang, the former mayor of Arcadia, Calif., exiting federal court after pleading guilty last month.

item.title: Infantino’s Yearslong Effort to Woo Trump for the World Cup
item.description: Gianni Infantino, the FIFA president, has unabashedly courted the president’s favor. Soccer officials privately ask, who really benefits?
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja and Rebecca R. Ruiz
item.media:credit: Anna Moneymaker/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump with Gianni Infantino, FIFA’s president, at the White House in 2019.
